title: Rumbledethumps
subtitle: Scottish potato, cabbage, and onion bake with a Cheddar crust
excerpt: Rumbledethumps is a Scottish dish from the Borders region: potato, cabbage, and onion combined and baked in a dish with a Cheddar crust. It is related to both bubble and squeak and colcannon but baked rather than fried, giving it a browned top and a soft, cohesive interior. It is a complete supper dish or a substantial side.
sourceNotes: Traditional Scottish Borders dish. F. Marian McNeill, The Scots Kitchen (1929): rumbledethumps as a standard Scottish Borders potato dish. Closely related to Irish colcannon and English bubble and squeak. Regional Scottish home cooking.

content.text: Rumbledethumps sits between bubble and squeak and a potato gratin: a mixture of mashed potato and softened cabbage and onion, baked with a Cheddar top until golden and bubbling. The name is the best thing about this dish's reputation; the food itself is
. It can be made with any combination of greens and can stand as a main dish or a side.

items.name: Potato
items.prepNote: floury variety, peeled and cut into chunks

content.text: Boil the {{potato}} potato chunks in salted water until tender, about 18 minutes. Drain and mash with half the {{unsalted-butter}}. Melt the remaining butter in a large pan, add the {{onion}} onion, and cook over a medium-low heat for 10 minutes until soft. Add the {{cabbage-savoy}} cabbage and cook for a further 5 minutes until wilted. Season everything with {{sea-salt-flakes}} and {{black-pepper}}.

Heat the oven to 180°C (160°C fan). Combine the mashed potato with the cabbage and onion mixture. Transfer to a buttered baking dish and spread evenly. Scatter {{cheddar}} of grated Cheddar over the top. Bake for 25 to 30 minutes until the cheese is golden-brown and the edges are bubbling. Rest for 5 minutes before serving.

rows.fix: Increase the oven by 20°C for the final 5 minutes, or switch on the grill. Watch closely under the grill.
rows.cause: Cheese too thinly spread or oven not hot enough

content.text: Rumbledethumps is a Borders dish, from the part of Scotland closest to England, sharing culinary traditions with northern England as well as with the rest of Scotland. It is served as a supper dish or as a side with haggis, roast meat, or cold cuts. It is one of the less well-known Scottish regional dishes outside Scotland but is a standard of Borders home cooking.
